themes.exampleQuote: Please do not miss this hidden gem in Pittsburgh.
make sure you get the raspberry lychee macaroon, it is gluten free made with almond flour and it is the best thing I've ever put in my mouth hands down
Le Gourmandine can be put on any corner in Paris France and it would be packed.
there are multiple locations so please make sure you check it out

notable_quote: Le Gourmandine can be put on any corner in Paris France and it would be packed — I'm telling you this from experience.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers land overwhelmingly on reverence — Paris comparisons, superlatives about specific items like the raspberry lychee macaron — with almost no friction surfaced; nitpicks are absent entirely from the available sample, which itself is notable for a shop at 525 reviews.
quarter_narrative: Thirty independent Pittsburgh creators filmed La Gourmandine Hazelwood this quarter and generated over 1.09 million TikTok plays; the shop posted nothing to Instagram during that same window, going 40 days dark as of print. The city is making content about this place at a scale that most Pittsburgh businesses would architect a whole campaign to achieve, and La Gourmandine is capturing none of the downstream attention — no repost, no redirect, no trail back to the account. That gap is the whole story of Spring 2026 here. Meanwhile, Page's holds the Pittsburgh Sweets family lead on social momentum, a signal category where La Gourmandine ranks last in the family despite sitting at the top of the pastry-shop tier. Closing the distance between #6 in the family and #1 in Pittsburgh Sweets runs directly through the Instagram account that already exists and already has an audience — the question is whether it wakes up.
tldr_read: Paris-level review sentiment, a conversion path that works perfectly, and 1.09 million TikTok plays the business didn't generate itself — but a dormant Instagram drags the family rank to sixth; Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Pastry Shops.
tldr_meaning: Every signal axis except Instagram momentum is at or above family median, meaning the ceiling is already visible — the trajectory stalls until the account that has the audience starts using it.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Pittsburgh Put It On Paris-Level Footing. The Business Hasn't Posted In Forty Days.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram account now
playbook.action: The account has been silent for 40 days while 30 creators generated 1.09M TikTok plays; one weekly post reactivates the lowest-scoring signal in the family.

playbook.headline: Pull the TikTok wave into your own feed
playbook.action: Repost or story-share from the 30 creators already filming this shop — @ms__lesh alone has 719,500 plays — to redirect existing reach to a channel the business controls.

playbook.headline: Surface the raspberry lychee macaron visually
playbook.action: The most specific and emotionally charged item in the review record has no dedicated photo category on Google; a single tagged shoot adds to the 304-photo catalog and anchors the menu signal.
